name: Cloudcraft Cloud Architecture Diagram API
what_it_does: Cloud architecture diagramming tool with REST API for creating and managing AWS and Azure infrastructure diagrams — includes live sync feature that imports actual cloud resources from AWS accounts to generate current architecture diagrams automatically.
use_cases: Automatically generating architecture diagrams from live AWS/Azure resource data
Exporting infrastructure diagrams as PNG, PDF, or JSON for documentation agents
Creating diagram templates for new AWS deployments via API
Syncing live cloud infrastructure with diagrams for change detection workflows
Generating visual cost estimates by linking infrastructure diagrams to AWS pricing
not_for: General-purpose diagramming not focused on cloud architecture (use Miro or Lucidchart)
Self-hosted diagram management without cloud provider integration
Diagrams for non-cloud infrastructure (use draw.io for generic diagrams)
best_when: An agent generates or maintains architecture documentation that reflects live cloud infrastructure, especially for AWS environments.
avoid_when: You need general-purpose diagramming, self-hosted solutions, or multi-cloud diagrams beyond AWS/Azure.

auth.notes: API key from Cloudcraft account settings. Passed as Bearer token. Account-scoped access. AWS cross-account IAM role required for live sync feature.

pricing.paid_tiers: Pro $49/month (unlimited diagrams, AWS sync); Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: API access requires Pro plan or higher. Free tier is for UI-only use. Acquired by Datadog in 2019. Integrates with Datadog for infrastructure monitoring context.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits documented; AWS sync is rate-limited by AWS API calls

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documented with OpenAPI spec. Export endpoints well-documented (PNG, PDF, JSON formats). Live AWS sync requires AWS IAM setup. Error responses include codes and messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Diagram reads and exports are idempotent. Diagram creation generates new diagram each call. AWS sync updates existing diagram (idempotent with cloud state).
known_agent_gotchas: API access requires Pro plan — free tier users cannot use API
AWS live sync requires IAM cross-account role setup — not instant configuration
Diagram JSON format is proprietary — export to standard formats for interoperability
AWS resource sync rate-limited by AWS API quotas — large accounts may be slow

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 compliant (Datadog). Bearer token auth. TLS enforced. AWS IAM cross-account role with least privilege for sync. US data residency. Datadog-level security practices.
